title: An American Rate Bottleneck
summary:
Main
By George Lagarias
Jackson Hole, Wyoming , is a valley in North America, wholly unremarkable in history for anything other than it was named by someone called Jackson who caught bevers. Yet once a year, when the US Federal Reserve meets there, the eyes of the economic and investment world are fixed on this tiny area in the Rocky Mountains. The "Cary Trade Scare of 2024" is firmly behind us.
